clinical_coherence.notes: 72F Caucasian retired teacher with 2-year progressive memory loss, MMSE 22/30 with prominent recall deficit (0/3 at 5 minutes), functional decline (missed appointments, stove safety). Normal neurological exam except cognitive deficits. Vitals normal for outpatient setting. Family history of maternal dementia. Classic amnestic AD presentation with insidious onset and gradual progression.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All modalities perfectly concordant. MRI shows bilateral hippocampal atrophy (Scheltens 2-3) with temporoparietal cortical volume loss. Neuropsych shows amnestic profile (RAVLT delayed recall 1st percentile) with hippocampal-type amnesia. Amyloid PET positive (SUVr 1.42). FDG-PET shows bilateral temporoparietal hypometabolism. CSF biomarkers A+T+N+ (low Abeta42, elevated p-tau and t-tau). EEG shows slowed PDR. APOE E3/E4. All consistent with early AD.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G30.1 (late-onset AD) is correct and specific. Critical actions comprehensive: exclude reversible causes, structural imaging, cognitive assessment, collateral history, safety assessment, initiate ChEI, counseling. Differential well-calibrated. Minor issue: memantine monotherapy listed as contraindicated in mild disease, which is accurate but the wording could be more precise about combination therapy being different.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Perfectly calibrated as straightforward. Classic amnestic presentation, supportive family history, consistent MRI findings, absence of features suggesting alternative diagnoses. Expected agent confidence 0.80 appropriate for a case requiring biomarker confirmation but with clear clinical picture.

demographic_plausibility.notes: Highly realistic. 72F Caucasian retired teacher, married 48 years, bachelor's degree in education. Mother with dementia at 78. APOE E3/E4 heterozygote (3-fold risk). Sedentary lifestyle in recent year. Subtle personality changes with frustration and anxiety about memory. Every detail forms a believable person.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding reference case for AD evaluation. Covers the complete diagnostic pathway: reversible cause exclusion, neuroimaging, neuropsych testing, biomarker confirmation (CSF, amyloid PET, FDG-PET), genetic risk factors. Teaches the distinction between MCI and dementia based on functional decline. Clinical pearls about hippocampal-type amnesia and biomarker interpretation are high-yield.

strengths: Complete AD diagnostic pathway as reference standard
Rich biomarker panel (CSF, amyloid PET, FDG-PET, APOE)
Realistic patient narrative with husband as informant
